content.problemStatement:
A string t1t2…tmt_1 t_2 \ldots t_mt1​t2​…tm​ is called good if there is at most one position 1≤i<m1\le i < m1≤i<m such that ti≠ti+1t_i \ne t_{i+1}ti​=ti+1​. For example `aaabb` and `zzz` are good strings; while `abc` and `ssdddssds` are not good.

You are given a string sss, consisting of lowercase English letters.

Find the minimum kkk such that you can split sss into kkk good subsequences (a subsequence is not necessarily contiguous). Each character of sss should belong to exactly one of the kkk subsequences.

It can be shown that it is always possible to split sss into several subsequences this way.


Input Format

The only line of the input contains a string sss.


Output Format
Print a single integer −-− the answer to the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
First sample:
One of the splittings of aba into two good subsequences is:

First subsequence is a_a.
Second subsequence is _b_.

Second sample:
Since the string ddb is already good, the answer is 111.
Third sample:
One of the splittings of azazaza into two good subsequences is:

First subsequence is _za_a_a.
Second subsequence is a__z_z_.
